description: Replace manual PTY spawning with native Claude Code features: subagent definitions with worktree isolation, Agent SDK for programmatic control, hooks for safety/quality, auto permission mode, /loop for monitoring, persistent memory per slot. The cockpit becomes an orchestrator OF Claude Code sessions, not a PTY wrapper.
goals: Replace PTY spawn with Claude Code subagent definitions (isolation: worktree, background: true)
Generate .claude/agents/ files dynamically from chairman assignments
Use auto permission mode for full autonomy with safety classifier
Implement hooks for SafeExecutor, quality gates, and merge evaluation
Use /loop for heartbeat monitoring instead of custom polling
Enable persistent memory per agent slot across sessions
Cockpit drives Claude Code sessions via headless mode (-p flag)
non_goals: No Agent Teams (experimental, one team per session limit) — keep our own multi-slot system
No cloud scheduled tasks for v1 — local execution first
No Agent SDK rewrite of the full backend — incremental adoption

user_stories.title: Dynamic subagent definition generator
user_stories.description: When the cockpit assigns slots, auto-generate .claude/agents/ markdown files for each slot. Each file contains: name (slot-{N}-{skill}), description (from chairman taskDescription), tools (Read, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep — scoped by role), model (claude for complex, haiku for simple via cost routing), permissionMode (auto), isolation (worktree), memory (project), background (true), hooks (Stop hook to verify tests pass). The generator reads the chairman output + COP + PRD to produce rich agent definitions. Files written to {projectPath}/.claude/agents/. Existing files overwritten on each session activation.
tests: agent file generated for each slot assignment
isolation: worktree present in each file
tools scoped by slot role (backend gets Bash, frontend gets Edit)
Stop hook included for test verification

user_stories.title: Headless Claude Code launcher
user_stories.description: Replace direct PTY spawning with Claude Code headless mode. For each slot: launch `claude -p <prompt> --agent slot-{N}-{skill} --output-format stream-json --continue` in the slot's worktree. The prompt includes: AGENT.md content (chairman brief, task, stories, coordination rules). Output format stream-json gives structured events: assistant messages, tool calls, tool results, session_id. Parse the stream-json output to: route text to terminal slot view, detect tool calls for SafeExecutor, capture session_id for resume. Resume support: store session_id per slot, use `--resume <id>` on restart/recovery.
tests: claude launched in headless mode per slot
output routes to terminal slot view

user_stories.title: Hooks configuration for safety and quality
user_stories.description: Generate .claude/settings.json (or .claude/settings.local.json) with hooks configuration for each orchestration session. Hooks: 1) PreToolUse: block dangerous operations (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force push) — replaces our SafeExecutor pattern matching with Claude's native hook system. Use command type that checks against our 13 danger patterns. 2) PostToolUse on Edit|Write: trigger linter/formatter if configured. 3) Stop hook (agent type): spawns a verification subagent that checks if tests pass and all assigned stories are complete before allowing the slot to finish. If not done, returns reason as next instruction — agent keeps working. 4) SubagentStop: when a slot finishes, notify the cockpit to evaluate merge readiness.

user_stories.title: CLAUDE.md and rules generation
user_stories.description: Generate project-level .claude/CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/ files from the cockpit context. CLAUDE.md contains: project description, tech stack (detected from package.json/Cargo.toml), coding conventions (from existing CLAUDE.md or detected), orchestration rules (commit prefix, branch isolation, no cross-worktree changes). Rules directory: backend.md (path-scoped to src/api/**, models/**, services/**), frontend.md (path-scoped to src/components/**, src/views/**), testing.md (path-scoped to tests/**, spec/**). Each rule file loaded only when the agent works on matching paths — keeps context lean. Import system: CLAUDE.md uses @prd.json to auto-load PRD context.

user_stories.title: Persistent agent memory integration
user_stories.description: Each slot's subagent definition includes `memory: project`. This means Claude Code auto-maintains a MEMORY.md per agent that accumulates knowledge across sessions. Replace our custom AgentMemoryRepository with this native feature for the execution layer. The cockpit reads .claude/agents/slot-{N}/MEMORY.md to display learnings in the Intelligence panel. On session start, the cockpit can inject initial memories from our LearningRecord/PerformanceProfile data into the agent's memory: 'This agent performed well on backend_rust (94% success)' or 'Struggled with TypeScript generics last session'. Memory survives across sessions automatically — no custom persistence needed.
tests: initial memories injected from learning data

user_stories.title: Loop-based monitoring (/loop replacement)
user_stories.description: Instead of our custom heartbeat polling, use Claude Code's native /loop within each agent session. After launching the agent with the main task prompt, send a follow-up: '/loop 30s check git status and report progress in JSON format'. The agent periodically checks: git diff stat, test results, story completion, and outputs structured progress. Parse the loop output from the stream-json to update: slot progress bar, heartbeat status, story completion count. Alternatively: use a separate monitoring subagent (meta-monitor.md) with isolation: worktree set to false (works in main repo) that runs '/loop 1m check all worktrees for progress'.

user_stories.title: Session resume and recovery
user_stories.description: Claude Code headless mode returns session_id in stream-json output. Store this session_id per slot in the CockpitSession/AgentSlot DB record. On app restart or crash recovery: instead of re-launching from scratch, use `claude -p 'Continue where you left off' --resume <session_id> --output-format stream-json`. The agent resumes with full conversation history + file state. If session_id is expired (>24h), fall back to fresh launch with previous progress injected via AGENT.md. This replaces our custom crash recovery with Claude Code's native session persistence.
tests: expired session falls back to fresh launch

user_stories.title: Auto permission mode with cockpit safety overlay
user_stories.description: Set permissionMode: auto in subagent definitions. Claude's built-in safety classifier handles most decisions automatically. For additional XRoads-specific safety: configure PreToolUse hooks that check our SafeExecutor patterns. The cockpit UI shows a live feed of permission decisions from the auto classifier (approved/blocked) in the MCP LOGS panel. If the classifier blocks something the user wants to allow, they can update .claude/settings.local.json to whitelist specific patterns. The cockpit provides a UI to manage these allowlists without editing JSON manually.
tests: auto mode set in agent definitions
